10.1016/j.jmp.2025.102956
No data was used for the research described in the article.

Since the very beginning of its conceptualization and development  knowledge space theory  (KST; e.g.,  Doignon & Falmagne, 1985, 1999; Falmagne & Doignon, 2010; Heller & Stefanutti, 2024 ) was studied within a framework in which items had only two answer alternatives, namely  correct  and  wrong . In recent years, increasing research has focused on the generalization of  dichotomous KST  to the case of items with more than two answer alternatives. Such generalization to the  polytomous  case involved both the deterministic ( Heller, 2021; Schrepp, 1997; Stefanutti, Anselmi, de Chiusole, & Spoto, 2020 ) and the probabilistic ( Noventa, Spoto, Heller, & Kelava, 2019; Stefanutti, de Chiusole, Anselmi, & Spoto, 2020 ) parts of the theory. With respect to the latter, the generalization of the  basic local independence model  (BLIM; e.g.,  Falmagne & Doignon, 1988 ), that is the most widely probabilistic model in dichotomous KST, was developed and the  polytomous local independence model  (PoLIM;  Stefanutti, de Chiusole, Anselmi, & Spoto, 2020 ) was introduced.

Together with the generalization of some deterministic and probabilistic parts of the theory, the question is still pending whether some critical aspects of dichotomous KST are generalized to the polytomous case. Among these issues, in the present article we are going to explore whether, from a deterministic point of view, a generalization of the concepts of  forward  and  backward gradedness  (e.g.,  Spoto, Stefanutti, & Vidotto, 2012, 2013 ) exists and, if so, whether the identifiability issues of the BLIM when applied to  forward-  and/or  backward-graded  knowledge structures generalize to the PoLIM. Intuition suggests that gradedness in dichotomous structures should actually generalize to the polytomous case both in presence of an order among the different levels of the answer, and when no order exists among the multiple answering options of an item. On the other hand, establishing if identifiability issues exist in a generalization of gradedness to polytomopus structures appears to be much harder to predict. A positive answer to both these questions was found. The article provides, on the one hand, the formal definitions of the generalized concept of gradedness with its specific characteristics, and on the other hand, the proofs that the identifiability issues of the BLIM in graded dichotomous structures are a special case of the identifiability issues of the PoLIM for graded polytomous structures.

The paper is structured as follows. Section  2  introduces fundamental concepts of both the dichotomous and polytomous knowledge structure theory. It also provides an overview of the PoLIM, its assumptions, main equations, and its parameter space. Section  4  provides an extension of the notion of gradedness to the case of polytomous structures. This extension gives rise to an extended notion of “equally informative item” which is valid for polytomous knowledge structures. Section  5  is the central part of the article and presents the main results concerning the identifiability of the PoLIM with graded structures. This section is followed by Section  6 , which introduces a numerical example that illustrates various aspects related to the identifiability of PoLIM with graded structures. Section  7  shows how all the results concerning the identifiability of the BLIM for dichotomous structures are special cases of what is obtained here for polytomous structures. The article is closed in Section  8  by some final remarks and future perspectives.

In this section, the basic deterministic and probabilistic concepts of dichotomous and polytomous KST are presented. Moreover, the identifiability issues of the BLIM when applied to graded dichotomous structures are described.

Define the  knowledge domain  as the collection  
                            Q 
                          of items  
                            q 
                          that can be used to assess the knowledge of a specific topic. The collection  
                            
                               K

                               Q

                            K

                            0̸ 
                          and  
                            Q 
                         , is called a  knowledge structure  on the knowledge domain  
                            Q 
                         .

It has been shown ( Spoto et al., 2012, 2013; Stefanutti & Spoto, 2020; Stefanutti, Spoto, & Vidotto, 2018 ) that there is a close link between the deterministic part of a knowledge structure and the identifiability of the probabilistic model applied to it. In particular, the role of forward and backward gradedness of the structure in the identifiability of the BLIM has been widely studied. In the BLIM, which is a restricted latent class model, every point  
                            θ 
                          in the parameter space  
                            Θ

                               Q

                               B

                               Q

(2018) , the transformations that preserve the prediction on the response patterns by modifying the values of the parameters are introduced and named, respectively, forward and backward parameter transformations. Here we focus on the former which is provided in  Stefanutti et al. (2018)  and reported here for convenience.

                         . Along the same lines, analogous transformation and interval are defined for BG knowledge structures.

The notion of equally informative items is particularly important in the context of identifiability of the BLIM for FG and BG structures. Two items  
                            
                               p 
                               , 
                               q 
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                            
                          are said to be  equally informative  with respect to a knowledge structure  
                            
                               ( 
                               Q 
                               , 
                               K 
                               ) 
                            
                          if  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                               
                               = 
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                               
                            
                         . It has been shown that if two or more items are equally informative, then the knowledge structure can be neither BG nor FG in any of those items ( Spoto et al., 2013 ). Notably, equally informative items prevent cases of unidentifiability due to either forward or backward gradedness, which are by far the most frequent sources of unidentifiability in the BLIM ( Spoto et al., 2013 ).

The theory of knowledge structures has been recently extended to items having more than two answer alternatives (henceforth called  polytomous items ) both for its deterministic ( Stefanutti, Anselmi, de Chiusole, & Spoto, 2020 ) and probabilistic ( Stefanutti, de Chiusole, Anselmi, & Spoto, 2020 ) aspects. The fundamental concepts of the theory have been generalized as follows. The collection of possible response values for the items is denoted  
                            V 
                         , with, obviously,  
                            
                               
                                  | 
                                  V 
                                  | 
                               
                               ≥ 
                               2 
                            
                         . Depending on the type of application and on the nature of the polytomous items, there can be some relations among the response values in  
                            V 
                         . For example, with items on the rating scale, the response values in  
                            V 
                          may be linearly ordered. In other applications, the order may also be partial ( Heller, 2021 ), or there could be no order at all ( Stefanutti, de Chiusole, Anselmi, & Spoto, 2020 ). The theoretical results presented in this paper do not depend in any way on the specific relation assumed on the set  
                            V 
                         .

An observable polytomous response pattern is represented by a mapping  
                            
                               R 
                               : 
                               Q 
                               → 
                               V 
                            
                         , where  
                            Q 
                          is, as usual, the set of items. The collection of all polytomous response patterns is denoted  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         . An alternative way of representing the response patterns is as a subset of  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               V 
                            
                         , that is, as a  
                            
                               
                                  | 
                                  Q 
                                  | 
                               
                               − 
                            
                         tuple  
                            
                               R 
                               = 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                     
                                        1 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                     
                                        2 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  … 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        v 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           | 
                                           Q 
                                           | 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                         . A  polytomous structure  is a triple  
                            
                               ( 
                               Q 
                               , 
                               V 
                               , 
                               K 
                               ) 
                            
                         , where  
                            K 
                          is a non-empty subset of  
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         . When an order exists among the values in  
                            V 
                          it is sometimes assumed that  
                            V 
                          contains a least and a greatest element, denoted, respectively,  
                            ⊥ 
                          and  
                            ⊤ 
                         . In this case, it is also assumed that the polytomous structure contains at least the state  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               
                                  { 
                                  ⊥ 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                          and the state  
                            
                               Q 
                               × 
                               
                                  { 
                                  ⊤ 
                                  } 
                               
                            
                         . Every mapping  
                            
                               K 
                               ∈ 
                               K 
                            
                          is called a  polytomous state .

                                        a 
                                        , 
                                        b 
                                        , 
                                        c 
                                        } 
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  K 
                                as displayed by the Hasse diagram in  Fig. 1 , where the three response categories in  
                                  V 
                                are ordered by  
                                  ≺

                                     a 
                                     ≺ 
                                     b 
                                     ≺ 
                                     c 
                                  
                               .

                                     ( 
                                     Q

Like in dichotomous KST, also in its polytomous generalization the observed polytomous response pattern of an individual is distinct from his/her polytomous state, which is a latent construct. In the PoLIM, both sets  
                            Q 
                          and  
                            V 
                          are finite, and the relationship between the response pattern  
                            R 
                          and the knowledge state  
                            K 
                          is of a probabilistic nature. Under the assumption of local independence, the probability  
                            
                               P 
                               
                                  ( 
                                  R 
                                  | 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          of observing a response pattern  
                            R 
                          given the state  
                            K 
                          is modeled as follows. For every item  
                            
                               q 
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                            
                         , and every two response values  
                            
                               a 
                               , 
                               b 
                               ∈ 
                               V 
                            
                         , let  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     ϵ 
                                  
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  ( 
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ) 
                               
                               ∈ 
                               
                                  [ 
                                  0 
                                  , 
                                  1 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                          be a real valued parameter such that  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     ∑ 
                                  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     V

                                     q

                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ) 
                               
                               = 
                               1 
                            
                          for all  
                            
                               q 
                               ∈ 
                               Q 
                            
                          and all  
                            
                               a 
                               ∈ 
                               V 
                            
                         . Then,

                                        R 
                                        | 
                                        K 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     =

                                           q

                                        K

                                           q

Conditions 1 and 2 are obvious. On the other hand, Condition 3 is a generalization to polytomous items of the BLIM monotonicity condition  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     β

                         . The column monotonicity condition simply imposes that in the matrix  
                            
                               
                                  E

                          each diagonal element is the greatest within its own column.

The definition of forward and backward gradedness are well-defined for dichotomous knowledge structures, however they cannot be applied to polytomous structures. This fact is immediately evident as the polytomous states are not subsets of a given set  
                         Q 
                      , but mappings from that set to the set  
                         V 
                       of response values.

It was shown by  Heller (2021)  that, when the set  
                         V 
                       of levels is ordered by a relation  
                         ⪯ 
                      , any polytomous structure on a set  
                         Q 
                       of items and a set  
                         V 
                       of response values can be represented as a dichotomous structure on the set  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                       of “virtual items”, that is item response pairs  
                         
                            
                               ( 
                               q 
                               , 
                               v 
                               ) 
                            
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      . Let  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                       be the polytomous knowledge structure and for every pair  
                         
                            
                               ( 
                               p 
                               , 
                               v 
                               ) 
                            
                            ∈ 
                            Q 
                            × 
                            V 
                         
                      , define the collection  
                         
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        ↓ 
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     { 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        , 
                                        u 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     : 
                                     u 
                                     ⪯ 
                                     v 
                                     } 
                                  
                                  .

Then, for every polytomous state  
                         
                            K 
                            : 
                            Q 
                            → 
                            V 
                         
                       the collection  
                         
                            
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∗ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        ⋃ 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           p 
                                           , 
                                           v 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        K 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        ↓ 
                                        v 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            
                         
                      is a dichotomous representation of the polytomous state  
                         K 
                      . With these definitions, the collection  
                         
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ∗ 
                               
                            
                            = 
                            
                               { 
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                               : 
                               K 
                               ∈ 
                               K 
                               } 
                            
                         
                       is a dichotomous representation of the polytomous knowledge structure. It is important to clarify that  
                         
                            
                               K 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       is not a dichotomous knowledge structure according to the canonical definition (see, for instance,  Doignon & Falmagne, 1999 ), since it misses the empty set. A sufficient condition for having a knowledge structure is that the set  
                         V 
                       contains a minimum ( 
                         ⊥ 
                      ) with respect to the order relation  
                         ⪯ 
                      . In this case, in fact, it is sufficient to apply the procedure described above to  
                         Q 
                       and  
                         
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  + 
                               
                            
                            = 
                            V 
                            ∖ 
                            ⊥ 
                         
                      . In that case, the obtained representation  
                         
                            
                               K 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       is a dichotomous knowledge structure on the set  
                         
                            Q 
                            × 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  + 
                               
                            
                         
                       in the canonical sense. Then there is an order isomorphism from  
                         K 
                       to  
                         
                            
                               K 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       ( Heller, 2021 ).

Once the polytomous structure is transformed into a dichotomous one according to this last procedure, forward and backward gradedness can be checked in the usual way.

Example 2

                                  × 
                                  V

                                  ( 
                                  p 
                                  , 
                                  v 
                                  )

                                     p 
                                  
                                  
                                     ↓ 
                                     v

                                  Q 
                                  × 
                                  
                                     
                                        V 
                                     
                                     
                                        + 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Moreover, it is easily checked that the dichotomous structures in  Fig. 2  are isomorphic to the polytomous structure in  Fig. 1 .

                                  p 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  )

As it will be detailed in the next section, when the knowledge structure is polytomous, the notions of forward and backward gradedness are a special case of a more general type of gradedness. This last is at the basis of many sources of unidentifiability of the PoLIM which are not captured by forward and backward gradedness alone.

In this section the notion of gradedness is generalized to polytomous structures.

Definition 2 
                         A polytomous knowledge structure

                                                       if 
                                                       
                                                          
                                                             q 
                                                          
                                                          
                                                             ′ 
                                                          
                                                       
                                                       ≠ 
                                                       q 
                                                       . 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               
                            for all  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            .

Example 3 
                         Considering again  Example 1 , the structure  
                               K 
                             is graded in both items  
                               p 
                             and  
                               q 
                             for various ordered pairs of response values. Concerning item  
                               p 
                            , there is a gradedness for the pair  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ) 
                               
                            . In fact, there are two states assigning response value  
                               a 
                             to item  
                               p 
                            , namely  
                               
                                  a 
                                  a 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  a 
                                  b 
                               
                            . Then, the gradedness is due to the existence in  
                               K 
                             of both states  
                               
                                  b 
                                  b 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  b 
                                  a 
                               
                            . Still for item  
                               p 
                             there are other three pairs of response values for which the structure is graded:  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  b 
                                  , 
                                  c 
                                  ) 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  c 
                                  ) 
                               
                            , and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  b 
                                  , 
                                  a 
                                  ) 
                               
                            . Moreover, the structure is graded in  
                               q 
                             for the pairs  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ) 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  b 
                                  , 
                                  a 
                                  ) 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  c 
                                  , 
                                  a 
                                  ) 
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  c 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ) 
                               
                            . In the whole, the polytomous structure at issue presents gradedness for eight different pairs of response categories. This is a totally different situation, compared to the results obtained by applying the procedure described in the previous section. It will be shown later that backward and forward gradedness are special cases of gradedness.

Considering again  Example 1 , the structure

In Section  3  it was shown that a polytomous structure and its dichotomous representation are isomorphic. It is thus possible to obtain the polytomous structure corresponding to the dichotomous structure by setting  
                         
                            V 
                            = 
                            
                               { 
                               0 
                               , 
                               1 
                               } 
                            
                         
                       and then test if the former is graded in the sense of  Definition 2 , and for which items.

                            By applying the procedure described in the previous section, we observed that the dichotomous representation  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗

                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗

                               a 
                             and its immediate successor  
                               b 
                            , while backward gradedness refer to the maximum level  
                               c 
                             and its immediate predecessor  
                               b 
                            . All the other cases, including gradedness between non contiguous levels (i.e.,  
                               a 
                             and  
                               c 
                            ), are captured by neither forward nor backward gradedness.

The next theorem follows at once from  Definition 2 .

Theorem 3 
                         
                            Given any item  
                            
                               
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                            , define the binary relation  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     ≾ 
                                  
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                               
                             
                            on the set  
                            
                               V 
                             
                            such that, given any two levels  
                            
                               
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ∈ 
                                  V 
                               
                            
                            ,  
                            
                               
                                  a 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≾ 
                                     
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                  
                                  b 
                               
                             
                            if the polytomous structure  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is graded in  
                            
                               q 
                             
                            for the ordered pair  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ) 
                               
                            
                            . Then  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     ≾ 
                                  
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                               
                             
                            is reflexive and transitive, that is a quasi-order on the set  
                            
                               V 
                             
                            of levels.

Given any item

                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        c 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . Therefore  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        a 
                                        c 
                                     
                                  
                                  ⊆ 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                     
                                        c 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            . This shows transitivity. Reflexivity: it suffices to notice that the mapping  
                               
                                  
                                     g 
                                  
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     a 
                                  
                               
                             maps  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                                  
                                     a 
                                  
                               
                             onto itself. Therefore  
                               
                                  a 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≾ 
                                     
                                     
                                        q 
                                     
                                  
                                  a 
                               
                             follows. □

Example 6 
                         For the polytomous structure of  Example 1 , the directed graphs of the two quasi-order relations  
                               
                                  
                                     ≾ 
                                  
                                  
                                     p 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               
                                  
                                     ≾ 
                                  
                                  
                                     q 
                                  
                               
                             are illustrated in  Fig. 3  (where the arcs for the reflexive pairs are omitted). As expected, the two relations are transitive (and reflexive). Moreover, we observe that they are not antisymmetric. As already seen,  
                               K

                               p 
                             for both

                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ) 
                               
                             and

                                  a

                            , implying that both

                                        ≾

                                  b

                                        p 
                                     
                                  
                                  a

                                  a 
                                  ≠ 
                                  b 
                               
                            . It is a consequence of this and of  Theorem 2  that, in this example,  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K

Equally informative items have an important role in dichotomous KST. In particular, as explained in Section  2 , a dichotomous knowledge structure cannot be (forward or backward) graded in an item if this last belongs to a set of equally informative items. A similar result can be obtained with polytomous structures. First of all, an extension of equal informativeness to polytomous items is needed. Such an extension was provided by  Stefanutti, Anselmi, de Chiusole, and Spoto (2020) . That definition is further generalized here to a weaker form of equal informativeness.

Definition 3 
                         Two items  
                               
                                  p 
                                  , 
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                             are said  equivalent  (written  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ≈ 
                                  q 
                               
                            ) if there is a nonempty subset  
                               
                                  T 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  V 
                               
                             and an injective mapping  
                               
                                  f 
                                  : 
                                  T 
                                  → 
                                  V 
                               
                             such that, for every  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  f 
                                  ∘ 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            .

Two items  
                               
                                  p 
                                  , 
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                             are said  equivalent  (written  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ≈ 
                                  q 
                               
                            ) if there is a nonempty subset  
                               
                                  T 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  V 
                               
                             and an injective mapping  
                               
                                  f 
                                  : 
                                  T 
                                  → 
                                  V 
                               
                             such that, for every  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            ,  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  f 
                                  ∘ 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            .

The following theorem is an almost immediate consequence of  Definition 3 . For completeness, a proof is provided anyway.

Theorem 4 
                         
                            Two items  
                            
                               
                                  p 
                                  , 
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                             
                            are equivalent if and only if there are nonempty subsets  
                            
                               
                                  T 
                                  , 
                                  U 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  V 
                               
                             
                            and a bijective function  
                            
                               
                                  h 
                                  : 
                                  T 
                                  → 
                                  U 
                               
                             
                            such that, for every  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            
                            ,  
                            
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  h 
                                  ∘ 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            
                            .

Two items  
                            
                               
                                  p 
                                  , 
                                  q 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Q 
                               
                             
                            are equivalent if and only if there are nonempty subsets  
                            
                               
                                  T 
                                  , 
                                  U 
                                  ⊆ 
                                  V 
                               
                             
                            and a bijective function

                                  T 
                                  →

                                  =

                                  T

                                     v

                                  f

                                     v

                               f

                               h

                             and  
                               U

                                     q

                                  h

                                     p

                            . □

                                  U 
                                  =

                                  h

                                     v

                                     ( 
                                     v 
                                     )

                               f 
                             is injective,  
                               h 
                             is bijective between  
                               T 
                             and  
                               U 
                            . To show that  
                               h 
                             satisfies the equality  
                               
                                  K

                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  h 
                                  ∘ 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     )

It should be observed that, although a state  
                         
                            K 
                            ∈ 
                            K 
                         
                       may assign different values to two equally informative items, once the value is determined for one of the two, the value of the other is also determined. This is what makes two items “equally informative”. We also observe that “equal informativeness” as defined in  Stefanutti, Anselmi, de Chiusole, and Spoto (2020)  is the special case of item isomorphism where the mapping  
                         f 
                       is the identity.

Theorem 5 
                         
                            Suppose that  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a polytomous knowledge structure where  
                            
                               p 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               q 
                             
                            are equivalent. Then  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            can neither be graded in  
                            
                               p 
                             
                            nor in  
                            
                               q 
                            
                            .

Suppose that  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  Q 
                                  , 
                                  V 
                                  , 
                                  K 
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            is a polytomous knowledge structure where  
                            
                               p 
                             
                            and  
                            
                               q 
                             
                            are equivalent. Then  
                            
                               K 
                             
                            can neither be graded in  
                            
                               p 
                             
                            nor in  
                            
                               q 
                            
                            .

Proof 
                         Let  
                               
                                  f 
                                  : 
                                  T 
                                  → 
                                  U 
                               
                             be the bijection as in  Theorem 4 . Given any two levels  
                               
                                  a 
                                  , 
                                  b 
                                  ∈ 
                                  T 
                               
                            , and any  
                               
                                  K 
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                            , if  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  a 
                               
                             then  
                               
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  f 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     a 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . Suppose there is  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ∈ 
                                  K 
                               
                             with  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        a 
                                        → 
                                        b 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            , so that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  b 
                               
                            . Then, since  
                               
                                  p 
                                  ≈ 
                                  q 
                               
                            , we have  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  f 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     
                                        
                                           K 
                                        
                                        
                                           ′ 
                                        
                                     
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        p 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  f 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     b 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  ≠ 
                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     q 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                  = 
                                  f 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     a 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            , because  
                               f 
                             is a bijection. This contradicts  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  = 
                                  
                                     
                                        K 
                                     
                                     
                                        p 
                                     
                                     
                                        a 
                                        → 
                                        b 
                                     
                                  
                               
                            , hence  
                               K 
                             cannot be graded in  
                               p 
                            . For analogous reasons,  
                               K 
                             cannot even be graded in  
                               q 
                            . □

Let

                                  K 
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     p 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               
                            . It can be checekd that  
                               
                                  
                                     K 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                             is no longer graded in item  
                               p 
                             (nor in item  
                               r 
                            ). On the other hand, concerning gradedness, things remain the same for what concerns item  
                               q 
                            .

It is shown in this section that the PoLIM is not identifiable when it is applied to graded polytomous structures. A  PoLIM instance  is a 4-tuple  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            , 
                            θ 
                            ) 
                         
                       where  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            ) 
                         
                       is a polytomous structure and  
                         
                            θ 
                            ∈ 
                            Θ 
                         
                       is a point in the parameter space of the PoLIM. Two distinct PoLIM instances  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            , 
                            θ 
                            ) 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ′ 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  θ 
                               
                               
                                  ′ 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       are said to be equivalent if the equality  
                         
                            
                               
                                  φ 
                               
                               
                                  R 
                               
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               θ 
                               ) 
                            
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  φ 
                               
                               
                                  R 
                               
                               
                                  ′ 
                               
                            
                            
                               ( 
                               
                                  
                                     θ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ′ 
                                  
                               
                               ) 
                            
                         
                       holds for all  
                         
                            R 
                            ∈ 
                            
                               
                                  V 
                               
                               
                                  Q 
                               
                            
                         
                      , where  
                         φ 
                       and  
                         
                            
                               φ 
                            
                            
                               ′ 
                            
                         
                       are, respectively, the prediction function of the two PoLIM instances. In the case  
                         
                            K 
                            = 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ′ 
                               
                            
                         
                      , which holds true throughout the present article, we say that the two points  
                         θ 
                       and  
                         
                            
                               θ 
                            
                            
                               ′ 
                            
                         
                       are equivalent whenever the two PoLIM instances  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            K 
                            , 
                            θ 
                            ) 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            ( 
                            Q 
                            , 
                            V 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  K 
                               
                               
                                  ′ 
                               
                            
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  θ 
                               
                               
                                  ′ 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       are equivalent.

Like in the dichotomous case, also for the graded polytomous structures, sufficient conditions for unidentifiability are found by following an approach based on mappings, named parameter transformations, that transform a given point in the model’s parameter space to another point which predicts identical distribution on the observable response patterns.

Theorem 6 
                         
                            Given any pair  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     (

Corollary 1 
                         
                            For every point  
                            
                               
                                  θ 
                                  ∈ 
                                  Θ 
                               
                             
                            in the parameter space of the PoLIM a neighborhood  
                            
                               
                                  N

In order to clarify all the concepts introduced in the previous section on PoLIM identifiability in the case of a graded structure, here we analyze the application of the described transformations to the parameters of a small graded polytomous structure. In particular, let us consider the polytomous structure  
                         K 
                       of  Example 1 , where  
                         
                            Q

                            ( 
                            b

                            c

                            a
